pageSubTitle: A way to support multiple languages (I also don't know how to translate all this into French so bear with me and my 'fr version' tags)
LangLinkExplanation: (fr version) LangLink is the replacement for most of your page-traveling <a> tags. Works just like a normal a tag, keeps all the attributes, just a slight name change for some redirect logic to preserve the language.
relativeLinkExplanation: (fr version) In some cases, you have a link to a child page. In this instance, you don't need a full absolute link like with LangLink, and can just use a normal relative link. DON'T prepend it with '/'
textVariablesExplanation: (fr version) You can replace words in your strings like this : {variableWord}

nestedText.explanation: (fr version) You can format and pull data from the language file like any normal .json

changeLangExplanation: (fr version) Clicking links like these basic ones will switch the lang and redirect to homepage.
addLang: (fr version) How to add additional languages
addLangExplanation: (fr version) To add more languages, add the lang code to src/lib/i18n/loadTranslations.js, and then add the language to the pages with that new code, like 'es.json'. Make sure the json headers match between all files.
